z,Checker for CherryPy sites and mounted apps.
d d!
d"d#
d$d%
d&d'
d(d)
d*d+
d,d-
Checkera
A checker for CherryPy sites and their mounted applications.
    When this object is called at engine startup, it executes each
    of its own methods whose names start with ``check_``. If you wish
    to disable selected checks, simply add a line in your global
    config which sets the appropriate method to False::
        [global]
        checker.check_skipped_app_config = False
    You may also dynamically add or replace ``check_*`` methods in this way.
    Tc
Initialize Checker instance.N)
_populate_known_types)
self
7/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/cherrypy/_cpchecker.py
__init__
Checker.__init__c
Run all check_* methods.Z
check_
__call__N)
warnings

startswith

oldformatwarning
name
methodr

Checker.formatwarningFc

The application mounted at %r contains the following config entries, which are only allowed in site-wide config. Move them to a [global] section and pass them to cherrypy.config.update() instead of tree.mount().N)

z3Check for mounted Applications that have no config.z2The Application mounted at %r has an empty config.z
 It looks like the config you passed to cherrypy.config.update() contains application-specific sections. You must explicitly pass application config via cherrypy.tree.mount(..., config=app_config)N)

z Checker.check_skipped_app_configc
z?Check for App config with extraneous brackets in section names.
The application mounted at %r has config section names with extraneous brackets: %r. Config *files* need brackets; config *dicts* (e.g. passed to tree.mount) do not.N)
endswithr

z4Check Application config for incorrect static paths.z
/dummy.htmlz
tools.staticdir.onFr
tools.staticdir.rootz
tools.staticdir.dirNz
tools.staticdir.dir is not set.z8dir is an absolute path, even though a root is provided.
If you meant to serve the filesystem folder at %r, remove the leading slash from dir.z,dir is a relative path and no root provided.z
%r is not an absolute path.
z3%r (root + dir) is not an existing filesystem path.z!%s

zAAssert that config values are of the same type as default values.N)
check_config_types2
Checker.check_config_typesc
z1Warn if any socket_host is 'localhost'. See #711.z
server.socket_hostZ	localhostz
The use of 'localhost' as a socket host can cause problems on newer systems, since 'localhost' can map to either an IPv4 or an IPv6 address. You should use '127.0.0.1' or '[::1]' instead.N)
check_localhost;
Checker.check_localhost)
